steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to 'emphasize the size of the sculpture.' Generic words like 'large' or 'monumental' are weak — concrete measurements make scale vivid. The notes give exact numbers: Cycle is roughly 62 feet wide and 56 feet tall.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'describes the sculpture in general terms': Uses vague descriptors without dimensions, so the reader gets no real sense of scale. ✗
  • Choice B — 'includes both specific measurements and uses 'stretches' to convey spatial extent': Supplies the 62-foot and 56-foot figures and uses active language ('stretches') to help the reader feel the size. ✓
  • Choice C — 'focuses on materials rather than dimensions': Mentions what the sculpture is made of, not how big it is — doesn't address size. ✗
  • Choice D — 'emphasizes artistic technique without giving measurements': Centers on how the work was created, not its physical scale. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
